title: dryopteris affinis subsp. atropes
intro: Dryopteris affinis subsp. atropurpurea, also known as autumn fern, is a type of deciduous fern native to East Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving menstrual cramps, and protecting against cardiovascular disease. Dryopteris affinis subsp. atropurpurea is often used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to promote overall well-being.
